flashcards.front: What is the heap property in a max-heap?
flashcards.back: Parent nodes are always greater than or equal to their child nodes.

flashcards.front: What is the time complexity of extracting the maximum element from a max-heap?
flashcards.back: O(log n) due to the heapify-down operation.

flashcards.front: How is a binary heap typically represented in memory?
flashcards.back: As an array where children of node i are at 2i+1 and 2i+2.
All levels except possibly the last are completely filled, and nodes are left-justified.

flashcards.front: What operation maintains the heap property after insertion?
Why are heaps preferred for priority queue implementation?
flashcards.back: They provide O(1) access to max/min element and O(log n) insertion/deletion.

flashcards.front: What is the height of a heap with n elements?
What algorithm uses heaps for efficient sorting?
flashcards.back: Heap Sort with O(n log n) time complexity.

flashcards.front: What is the difference between max-heap and min-heap?
flashcards.back: Max-heap has parent ≥ children, min-heap has parent ≤ children.

flashcards.front: What is the time complexity of building a heap from an array?
How does a priority queue differ from a regular queue?
flashcards.back: Elements are processed by priority rather than FIFO order.

flashcards.front: What real-world application uses priority queues?
